steps.title: Identify the modifier
steps.content: The sentence opens with Recordings of electrical activity in the brain, — an appositive that must rename the noun that immediately follows. The subject after the comma must be a thing that is recordings of brain activity.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
Electrograms are recordings of electrical activity in the brain — so electrograms must be the subject right after the comma. Choice A — electrograms show that while responding to hypothetical match scenarios, the most highly skilled soccer players have places electrograms there and produces a grammatical sentence: ...electrograms show that...the most highly skilled soccer players have increased activity...

B, C, and D place a different noun after the appositive, creating a misplaced modifier.

That matches Choice A — electrograms show that while responding to hypothetical match scenarios, the most highly skilled soccer players have.
